channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Building AI Evaluation Pipelines: Automating LLM Testing from Dataset to CI/CD
item.description: Part 2 of a series on testing AI systems in production In Part 1, we explored why testing AI systems is fundamentally different from traditional software. We talked about non-determinism, prompt...

item.title: Function Calling Harness 2: CoT Compliance from 9.91% to 100%
item.description: TL;DR 9.91% is not "did the model get it right on the first try" — it's "did the model walk through the procedure to the end." Even a frontier model can fail a simple constraint like "don't skip...

item.title: The Last Programming Language You'll Ever Learn Is English
item.description: There's a saying going around that hits a little too close to home. 2024's programming language is Solidity. 2025's is Python. 2026's is English. If that doesn't make you pause, read it again. We...

item.title: Fine-tuning YOLOv11 to detect stamps and signatures on banking documents - a practical walkthrough
item.description: Every day, banking ops teams manually review thousands of documents - loan applications, KYC forms, contracts - looking for the right stamps, the right signatures, in the right places. It's slow,...

item.title: Fine-tuning YOLOv11 to detect stamps and signatures on banking documents - a practical walkthrough
item.description: Every day, banking ops teams manually review thousands of documents - loan applications, KYC forms, contracts - looking for the right stamps, the right signatures, in the right places. It's slow,...

item.title: Paper 142 — 11-Platform Retrofit, PhilPapers Integration & Predatory Journal Defense (Rei-AIOS)
item.description: This article is a re-publication of Rei-AIOS Paper 142 for the dev.to community. The canonical version with full reference list is in the permanent archives below: GitHub source (private):...

item.title: AI Agents Authentication: How Autonomous Systems Prove Identity
item.description: TL;DR: AI agents are acting entities given more and more autonomy to execute tasks, write code, and integrate with SaaS tools. To do so, they need to authenticate with numerous systems, making AI...

item.title: High-Performance Caching — Redis, CDNs, DNS & The Cache-Aside Pattern (2026)
item.description: Optimizing Performance with Caching Strategies As developers, we strive to create scalable and efficient systems. After refining our database models and normalizing schemas, we must address the...

item.title: You’re Using VS Code Wrong (7 Extensions That 10x Your Productivity)
item.description: You’re already using Visual Studio Code. But here’s the uncomfortable truth: Most developers use only 20% of its power. And that gap? That’s the difference between: ❌ Constant context switching ✅...

item.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
item.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS...

item.title: Performance Improvements Don't Last. Here's Why
item.description: A team spends a sprint optimizing LCP. Numbers improve. Six months later the app is slower than before the work started. Nobody made a single decision to make it slower. It just accumulated. This...

item.title: How to win — and lose — Decoder
item.description: This interview has been lightly edited for length and clarity. Hello and welcome to Decoder, Nilay’s show about big ideas and other problems. This is Nick Statt, senior producer, and I’m joined by...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: SMS Blaster Busts, OpenEMR Flaws, 600K Roblox Hacks and 25 More Stories
item.description: The internet is noisy this week. We are seeing some wild new tactics, like people using fake cell towers to send scam texts, while some developers are accidentally downloading tools that peek into...

item.title: Samsung says the RAM shortage could get even worse next year
item.description: There may be a long wait before the end of the RAM shortage that's driving up prices on everything from phones to gaming handhelds. During an earnings call on Thursday, Samsung predicted that the...

item.title: The Commodore 64 and ZX Spectrum have been turned into retrofuturistic handhelds
item.description: Blaze Entertainment, the company behind the cartridge-based Evercade consoles, has announced two new handhelds that reimagine iconic '80s personal computers as portable gaming machines. The...

item.title: OpenAI talks about not talking about goblins
item.description: OpenAI is opening up about its goblin problem. After a report from Wired revealed instructions to OpenAI's coding model to "never talk about goblins, gremlins, raccoons, trolls, ogres, pigeons, or...

item.title: Verified by Spotify badge lets you know this artist isn’t AI
item.description: Spotify is launching a new verification program to combat spam, fakes, and AI. Some artists will now have a "Verified by Spotify" badge and a green checkmark on their profile, indicating that the...

item.title: Here’s what Netflix’s new vertical video feed is like
item.description: Netflix is starting to roll out a big revamp of its mobile app, and a central feature is a new vertical video feed called "Clips" where you can scroll through clips of shows, movies, and other...

item.title: New Python Backdoor Uses Tunneling Service to Steal Browser and Cloud Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a stealthy Python-based backdoor framework called DEEP#DOOR that comes with capabilities to establish persistent access and harvest a wide range...

item.title: The Download: the North Pole’s future and humanoid data
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Digging for clues about the North Pole’s past In the past,...

item.title: Meta says its business AI now facilitates 10 million conversations a week
item.description: Meta said over 8 billion advertisers have used at least one of its gen AI tools

item.title: Instagram says it doesn’t want your tweet round ups
item.description: The internet is full of copycat, stolen, reposted, and low-effort content - and Meta, at least publicly, has said it is working to cut off some of the reach. Beginning in 2024, the company has...

item.title: Is this ‘de-extinction’ project actually onto something?
item.description: Dallas-based genetics and biotech startup Colossal has raised hundreds of millions of dollars from venture capitalists, the CIA, and Peter Thiel, among others. Its buzzy "de-extinction" projects...

item.title: Meta lost 20 million users last quarter
item.description: Meta is planning to pump billions more into AI investments this year, despite noting that millions of users have seemingly started to abandon its platforms. In an earning call on Wednesday, Meta...

item.title: EtherRAT Distribution Spoofing Administrative Tools via GitHub Facades
item.description: Intro A sophisticated, high-resilience malicious campaign was identified by Atos Threat Research Center (TRC) in March 2026. This operation specifically targets the high-privilege professional...

item.title: OpenAI’s new security model is for ‘critical cyber defenders’ only
item.description: OpenAI is preparing to launch a new frontier cybersecurity model, GPT-5.5-Cyber. CEO Sam Altman said the model will not be available to the general public, but will be first rolled out to a select...

item.title: These Men Allegedly Profit Off Teaching People How to Make AI Porn
item.description: Three Arizona women have filed a lawsuit against a group of men that alleges they used the women’s photos to make AI porn influencers, then offered online courses showing others how to do it.

item.title: Fast16 Malware
item.description: Researchers have reverse-engineered a piece of malware named Fast16. It’s almost certainly state-sponsored, probably US in origin, and was deployed against Iran years before Stuxnet: “…the Fast16...

item.title: New Linux 'Copy Fail' Vulnerability Enables Root Access on Major Distributions
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) flaw that could allow an unprivileged local user to obtain root. The high-severity vulnerability...

item.title: Reid Hoffman Thinks Doctors Should Ask AI for a Second Opinion
item.description: The LinkedIn cofounder now has an AI drug discovery startup—and thinks not asking chatbots for medical advice is “bordering on committing malpractice.”
